title: Mauerpark on a Sunday - Quick Summary
items: **Mauerpark is a Sunday thing.** The famous flea market and open-air karaoke both run on Sundays, on the former Berlin Wall death strip between Prenzlauer Berg and Wedding. Entry to the park, market and karaoke is **free**.
**Flea market: every Sunday 10:00-18:00**, Bernauer Straße 63. Come close to opening if you want the best vinyl, cameras and furniture, and **bring some cash** because not every seller takes cards.

**Bearpit Karaoke starts around 15:00** in good weather, roughly April to October. Joe Hatchiban runs it in the stone amphitheatre; it is **cancelled in rain and in winter**, so treat it as a happy probability, not a fixed schedule.
**Crowds peak 13:00-17:00.** For a calmer visit, arrive near 10:00 or after 17:30. For the full buzzing atmosphere, come early afternoon when market, food stalls and karaoke overlap.
**Getting there:** Tram M10 to Friedrich-Ludwig-Jahn-Sportpark or Wolliner Straße, U8 Bernauer Straße (~5 min walk), or U2 Eberswalder Straße from the south.
